mission.title: consul: Prebuilts for darwin/arm64 (M1 Apple Silicon)
mission.description: Prebuilts for darwin/arm64 (M1 Apple Silicon). Requested by 13+ users.

steps.title: Check current consul deployment
steps.description:
Verify your consul version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
helm list -n consul 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working consul installation.

steps.title: Review consul configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant consul configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
kubectl get configmap -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=consul
```
#### Feature Description

Generate prebuilds for darwin/arm64 and make available on consul.io website.

#### Use Case(s)

Allows easier use of consul on darwin/arm64 for M1 / apple silicon devices

steps.title: Apply the fix for Prebuilts for darwin/arm64 (M1 Apple Silicon)
steps.description:
Following up on a request from Jared Kirschner, this PR builds a new darwin arm64 binary. When this PR is merged to main or a release branch, the binary will be signed and notarized just like the other darwin binary.

An example binary built from this branch can be downloaded from github artifacts and smoke tested here: https://github.com/hashicorp/consul/actions/runs/1580741218

A note can be added to the changelog when the next release goes out saying that we will support darwin arm64

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
kubectl get events -n consul --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Prebuilts for darwin/arm64 (M1 Apple Silicon)" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Following up on a request from Jared Kirschner, this PR builds a new darwin arm64 binary. When this PR is merged to main or a release branch, the binary will be signed and notarized just like the other darwin binary.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with consul installed or the issue environment reproducible.
